caveats: Shared neutral humanoid reuse is local runtime scaffolding only until actor-specific Anny mesh, rig, hair/face, clothing, and animation evidence attaches.
actorHumanoidMaterializationContract.recommendedNextAction: materialize actor-specific Anny humanoid GLBs before treating visual role distinction as asset-level progress

caveats: Generic equipment reuse is local runtime scaffolding only until equipment-specific mesh/prefab, scale, placement, affordance, and scenario variant evidence attaches.
equipmentMaterializationContract.recommendedNextAction: materialize equipment-specific generated GLBs or prefabs before treating pediatric equipment as Quest, clinical, scoring, or production-ready

stationContext.title: Pediatric Asthma
stationContext.subtitle: Child-sized urgent-care bay with pulse oximeter, nebulizer setup, parent chair, and pediatric distraction items.
stationContext.initialVitals: HR 128, RR 32, SpO2 91% on room air
stationContext.interruption: Nurse Kevin Lee cue at 300s: oxygen request
stationContext.stageAriaLabel: Pediatric Urgent Care Bay station scene
stationContext.initialDialogueText: Maya Johnson: short sentences, scared, audible wheeze

dialogueTurns.text: Maya Johnson: Maya is using short sentences, wheezing audibly, and needs her breathing effort named before more questions. I am frightened; this response comes from the respiratory assessment requirement in the scenario definition.
Maya Johnson: The key inhaler history is that rescue inhaler ran out yesterday. I am frightened; this response comes from the respiratory assessment requirement in the scenario definition.

Maya Johnson: The likely trigger is that symptoms worsened after visiting a relative with cats. I am frightened; this response comes from the respiratory assessment requirement in the scenario definition.

Nurse Kevin Lee: Oxygen saturation is 91% on room air, so a clear oxygen request and reassessment plan should happen now. I am focused; this response comes from the pediatric escalation requirement in the scenario definition.

Nurse Kevin Lee: A nebulizer or bronchodilator plan should be stated clearly and checked back with the child and parent. I am focused; this response comes from the pediatric escalation requirement in the scenario definition.

Nurse Kevin Lee: If breathing effort or saturation worsens, the team needs an urgent escalation plan rather than reassurance alone. I am focused; this response comes from the pediatric escalation requirement in the scenario definition.

Tara Johnson: Tara can help once the plan is explained plainly: child_distress_validated, oxygen_plan_explained, parent_role_clarified. I am anxious; this response comes from the guardian communication requirement in the scenario definition.

Maya Johnson: Maya and Tara need a calm acknowledgement that fast breathing is scary before the next clinical step. I am frightened; this response comes from the guardian communication requirement in the scenario definition.

Nurse Kevin Lee: Patient note saved for faculty review with pediatric respiratory assessment, treatment response, and family communication documented. I am focused; this response comes from the patient note requirement in the scenario definition.
